demographics.living_situation: lives with partner, who is the primary carer

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: (spoken very quietly, mostly relayed by her partner) I pushed myself yesterday... now my body is lead, my brain is offline, and I can barely lift my head.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: She went out for a small outing yesterday and today she has completely crashed — she can hardly move, can't think straight, can't chew food, and can't stand any light or noise. Her partner is worried it is something more sinister and rang for advice.

symptom_narrative.onset: started overnight after a minor physical outing the previous day
symptom_narrative.duration: about a day so far and not lifting
symptom_narrative.character: crushing, paralytic, leaden fatigue with a 'brain offline' fog — slowed thinking and trouble finding words, everything feels like wading through concrete
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: the worst crash she has had — completely floored, can barely lift her head or speak in full sentences
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: whole body and 'my brain' — all over, both sides the same, not one side more than the other
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: constant since it started; came on the day after over-doing it
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: lying completely still in the dark and silence; nothing else helps
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: any exertion, light, noise, or trying to think or talk
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: slept 14 hours and woke up feeling no better at all; very sensitive to light and sound; feels her 'cells have run out of energy'
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: she gets these crashes after over-doing things — part of her long-standing post-viral condition — but this one is much more severe than usual
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: strictly bedbound today; cannot sit up unaided, eat solid food, or hold a conversation; depends on her partner for sips of fluid and to be turned

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: long-standing post-viral fatigue condition following COVID (has been told she has an ME/CFS-type picture)
gets 'crashes' after physical or mental over-exertion

current_medications_as_reported.name_as_patient_states: no new medicines; says she cannot tolerate stimulant-type medications

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: lives with her partner who is present and providing all of her care today (hydration, feeding, turning); highly dependent during the crash

objective_data_offered.value: partner reports she is awake and can whisper a few words but is utterly exhausted and keeps her eyes shut
her face looks symmetrical and both arms and legs move the same on each side — no drooping of one side of the face and no one-sided weakness, just very weak all over
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: a worried partner's visual impression over chat, not a neurological examination

objective_data_offered.value: she is wearing sunglasses in a blacked-out, silent room and flinches at any light or sound
she does not feel hot and has not had any shivering or chills; partner has been giving her small sips of electrolyte drink through a straw and she is keeping them down
she is still passing urine and has had a wee this morning
